summary: DHH recounts a positive customer support experience with Apple after his iBook battery died. Having previously had terrible support from Sony for a product bought outside Denmark, he was pleasantly surprised by Apple's efficient, Danish-speaking support that quickly arranged a replacement battery. The experience reinforced his enthusiasm for Apple products and he uses it to evangelize the Mac platform.
key_points: Apple's worldwide warranty worked seamlessly across borders, unlike DHH's prior Sony experience
The support interaction was fast, in his native language, and required no repeated personal information
DHH uses the positive experience as ammunition for his Apple evangelism, recommending iBooks and iMacs
Denmark's mandatory 2-year warranty law made foreign electronics purchases feel risky by comparison
money_quotes: World-wide Warranty actually means exactly that for Apple.
This has got to be one of the cleanest interactions I've ever had with any company on warranty issues.
I've been disillusioned with the support of electronics bought outside the Danish border.
What a surprise I got! Almost instant connection with a Danish-speaking supporter.
key_insight: Apple's seamless worldwide warranty support stood in sharp contrast to competitors and demonstrated that customer service done right becomes powerful word-of-mouth marketing.
